allocation_status: withheld: model_portfolio must record an exact identity

source_sections.references: [ErGr80] Erd\H{o}s, P. and Graham, R., Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number theory. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).
source_sections.remarks:
This sequence is sometimes called the Mian-Chowla sequence. It is trivial that this sequence grows at least like $\gg N^{1/3}$. 

Erd\H{o}s and Graham \cite{ErGr80} also asked about the difference set $A-A$, whether this has positive density, and whether this contains $22$. It does contain $22$, since $a_{15}-a_{14}=204-182=22$. The smallest integer which is unknown to be in $A-A$ is $33$ (see A080200). It may be true that all or almost all integers are in $A-A$.

This sequence is at OEIS A005282.

See also [156].

subproblem_targets.focus_question: Let $A=\{1,2,4,8,13,21,31,45,66,81,97,\ldots\}$ be the greedy Sidon sequence: we begin with $1$ and iteratively include the next smallest integer that preserves the Sidon property (i.e. there are no non-trivial solutions to $a+b=c+d$). What is the order of growth of $A$?
Is it true that\[\lvert A\cap \{1,\ldots,N\}\rvert \gg N^{1/2-\epsilon}\]for all $\epsilon>0$ and large $N$?
